title: Capsella oviculata
intro: Capsella oviculata, also known as shepherds' purse, is a small, annual plant belonging to the Brassicaceae family. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as wounds, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The bioactive constituents of Capsella oviculata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Capsella oviculata is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in ointments and poultices to treat skin conditions and wounds.
taxonomy: Capsella oviculata belongs to the family Brassicaceae, commonly known as the mustard or cabbage family. It is a species of flowering plant in the genus Capsella. Capsella oviculata is closely related to Capsella bursa-pastoris, which is a common weed. The exact classification of Capsella oviculata may be further refined through ongoing research in botany.
